content:
A 60 cm diameter well completely penetrates a confined aquifer of permeability $5 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. The length of the strainer (spanning the entire thickness of the aquifer) is 10 m . The drawdown at the well under steady state pumping is 1.0 m . Assume that the radius of influence for this pumping is 300 m .

The discharge from the well (in litres per minute) is ________ (round off to the nearest integer).
explanation:
To determine the discharge from a well that fully penetrates a confined aquifer, let's break down the given information and use the appropriate formula.

Given Parameters:

Permeability of the aquifer, $ k = 5 \times 10^{-4} \, \text{m/s} $

Thickness of the aquifer, $ b = 10 \, \text{m} $

Radius of influence, $ R = 300 \, \text{m} $

Radius of the well, $ r_w = 0.3 \, \text{m} $ (since the diameter is 60 cm)

Drawdown at the well, $ s_w = 1 \, \text{m} $

Formula Used:

The discharge $ Q $ from the well under steady state conditions can be calculated using the formula:

$ Q = \frac{2 \pi k b s_w}{\log_e\left(\frac{R}{r_w}\right)} $

Calculation:

Substitute the given values into the formula:

$ Q = \frac{2 \pi \times 5 \times 10^{-4} \times 10 \times 1}{\log_e\left(\frac{300}{0.3}\right)} = 4.54 \times 10^{-3} \, \text{m}^3/\text{s} $

To convert this discharge to litres per minute:

$ Q = 4.54 \times 10^{-3} \times 60 \, \text{litres/min} $

Final Result:

$ Q = 272.87 \, \text{litres/min} \approx 273 \, \text{litres/min} \text{ (rounded to the nearest integer)} $
